title: Aureline exact-build identity record
description: Boundary schema for the exact_build_identity_record frozen in docs/build/exact_build_identity_model.md. One identity covers binaries, symbols / source maps, docs packs, schema exports, SDK libraries, SBOM documents, signed attestations, source bundles, crash-symbol archives, reproducibility packs, and support-runbook bundles, so that the artifact graph, Help / About surfaces, release center, and support exports can all point at the same record without inventing per-family version strings. Raw binaries, raw symbol bodies, raw signing material, raw build logs, and raw SBOM bodies never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, digests, and timestamps only. The reproducible-build baseline record (schemas/build/build_identity.schema.json) is the minimal per-build identity; this record is the super-set every artifact family resolves a running_build_identity_ref into. Adding a new artifact-family class, release-channel class, tree-state class, toolchain field, target-tier class, cargo-profile class, debug-info class, panic class, strip class, build-epoch derivation class, producer-lane class, signing class, signing-material state, binary-embed class, hash algorithm, or evidence-link field is additive-minor and requires a schema version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

exact_build_identity_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this exact-build identity export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator. Every exact-build identity carries record_kind = exact_build_identity_record so parity audits and support exports can validate payloads mechanically.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests / release notes. Raw URLs, raw file paths, raw signing material, and raw build-log bodies never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

hash_algorithm_class.description: Frozen hash-algorithm vocabulary. sha1_git is the legacy git object hash kept for parity with the reproducible-build baseline record. sha256_git is the SHA-256 git object hash. sha256_content and blake3_content are content hashes used on artifacts that are not git objects (e.g. split debug info, SBOM body, signed attestation payload). Every hashed field carries an explicit algorithm token so a verifier never has to infer one.

product_name_class.description: Frozen product-name vocabulary. Only one product name is admissible today. The field exists so a parity audit can diff identities mechanically across future sibling products without re-keying the record shape.

release_channel_class.description: Frozen release-channel vocabulary. dev_local is a developer workstation build. nightly is the automated daily lane. preview is the gated pre-release lane that downstream surfaces render with the pre_release_unverified docs version-match state. beta is a publicly announced pre-release. stable is the general-availability lane. lts is a long-term-support branch on a stable line. hotfix is a patched release off a stable or lts line.

artifact_family_class.description: Frozen artifact-family vocabulary. ide_binary / cli_binary carry the platform executables; the matching *_debug_symbols family carries split / stripped debug info. source_map_bundle pairs bundled renderer output to sources. docs_pack is the docs-pack manifest (docs/docs/docs_pack_manifest_contract.md). schema_export is the versioned JSON Schema / SDL export. reference_pack is the generated-reference index. sbom_document is an SPDX / CycloneDX SBOM. signed_attestation is an in-toto / SLSA attestation payload. source_bundle is a distributable source archive. crash_symbols_archive is the symbolication archive consumed by the crash pipeline. reproducibility_pack is the clean-room rebuild instruction pack (milestones doc 'reproducibility pack'). support_runbook_bundle is the support-export runbook payload. release_evidence_packet is the aggregated public release-evidence envelope.

tree_state_class.description: Frozen tree-state vocabulary. clean_checkout is the only state admissible on beta / stable / lts / hotfix release lanes. dirty_local is admissible only on dev_local and explicitly rejected by ci_release / ci_hotfix lanes. patched_from_clean means a clean checkout had a listed set of named patches applied (used by reproducibility packs and offline mirror rebuilds). shallow_clone_accepted is admissible for dev_local only. archive_export_no_git covers a source_bundle rebuilt from a signed archive where git metadata is not available; committer_epoch is then derived from the signed archive header.

tree_state_rejected_reason_class.description: Frozen vocabulary for rejected tree states. When producer_lane.lane_class is a release lane and tree_state_class is not clean_checkout, at least one rejected reason MUST be recorded (for dev_local lanes this field is empty).

cargo_profile_class.description: Frozen cargo-profile vocabulary. dev / release mirror the baseline record; release_lto is the link-time-optimization release profile the release lane uses; bench is the benchmark-runner profile pinned by the bench council baseline. No other profile strings are admissible.

debug_info_class.description: Frozen debug-info vocabulary. no_debug_info is admissible only when a matching *_debug_symbols family ships separately. line_tables_only / limited_debug_info are admissible on release / release_lto. full_debug_info is admissible on dev. split_debug_info_external means debug info is stripped from the primary binary and shipped as a separate ide_debug_symbols / cli_debug_symbols artifact; a matching split_symbols_ref MUST be non-null.

panic_class.description: Frozen panic-strategy vocabulary. Fixed at build time and material to crash-symbolication.

strip_class.description: Frozen strip-class vocabulary. strip_symbols / strip_all require a matching *_debug_symbols artifact family cross-referenced via evidence.split_symbols_ref.

target_os_class.description: Frozen target-OS vocabulary. other_unspecified is admissible only on artifact families that are OS-agnostic (docs_pack, schema_export, reference_pack, sbom_document, reproducibility_pack, release_evidence_packet).

target_cpu_class.description: Frozen target-CPU vocabulary. cpu_agnostic is admissible only on artifact families that are CPU-agnostic (same list as target_os_class = other_unspecified).

target_tier_class.description: Frozen target-tier vocabulary. Mirrors the tier table in docs/build/reproducible_build_baseline.md. tier_not_applicable is used for OS/CPU-agnostic artifact families.

build_epoch_derivation_class.description: Frozen build-epoch derivation vocabulary. committer_timestamp_of_head is the default for dev_local / nightly / preview / beta. release_pipeline_pinned_epoch is the release lane's pinned epoch (must match the committer timestamp of the commit on release lanes). manually_pinned_epoch is admissible only on dev_local and reproducibility-pack replays. archive_header_pinned_epoch pairs with tree_state_class = archive_export_no_git.

producer_lane_class.description: Frozen producer-lane vocabulary. developer_local is the only lane that tolerates dirty_local trees or unsigned outputs. ci_preview / ci_merge_queue / ci_nightly / ci_beta / ci_release / ci_hotfix are the gated automated lanes. reproduced_clean_room is a verifier lane that rebuilds a prior identity; rebuild_of_identity_ref MUST be non-null. offline_mirror_rebuild is the air-gapped mirror lane; acquired_via on the downstream artifact MUST be air_gapped_media.

signing_class.description: Frozen signing-class vocabulary. unsigned_development_only is admissible only on developer_local. ephemeral_developer_signature is a non-release short-lived signature admissible on ci_preview / ci_merge_queue only. ci_transparent_sign is the transparency-log-backed signature used on ci_nightly / ci_beta. release_production_sign / release_hardware_key_sign are admissible on ci_release / ci_hotfix / reproduced_clean_room / offline_mirror_rebuild; the HSM-backed path must name the signing_material_state signed_verified. Final signing / notarization workflows are out of scope at this freeze; this axis pins only the vocabulary every artifact family references.

signing_material_state_class.description: Frozen signing-material state. Mirrors the docs-pack signature_status values. signed_verified is the only state that clears the release-evidence gate; signed_revoked is a publishable blocker on every downstream surface (release center, Help / About, public evidence packets).

binary_embed_class.description: Frozen vocabulary for how the identity is carried with the artifact. embedded_in_binary is the default for ide_binary / cli_binary / sdk_library (linked as a read-only symbol and projected by Help / About's copy-build-info). sidecar_manifest_beside_binary pairs with *_debug_symbols / source_map_bundle artifacts. external_registry_only is admissible on docs_pack / reference_pack / schema_export where the manifest is the primary carrier and the identity is reached through a registry resolver. embedded_in_archive_manifest is the source_bundle / reproducibility_pack / release_evidence_packet mode: the archive's top-level manifest carries the identity verbatim.

redaction_class.description: Frozen redaction-class vocabulary for this record. Matches the ADR-0007 / ADR-0008 redaction envelope. release_public is the only class that may appear verbatim in public release notes / About dialogs; support_export_only and evidence_packet_only may appear only under the matching export redaction frames.

policy_epoch.description: ADR-0008 policy epoch. Release lanes pin the policy epoch the build resolved settings against.

trust_state.description: ADR-0009 trust state of the producer context.

policy_context.description: Policy-context envelope re-exported from ADR-0008 / ADR-0009. Present on every record so downstream lanes can reason about the build's policy provenance without re-deriving it.

full_hash.description: Full VCS commit digest, lower-case hex. When tree_state_class is archive_export_no_git the value MUST be the synthetic digest minted by the archive-manifest signer.

short_hash.description: 12-character lower-case hex prefix of full_hash, convenience field for human-readable rendering. The rendering surface MUST NOT use this field for diffing; diffs use full_hash.

committer_epoch.description: Committer timestamp of the commit at HEAD, in seconds since the Unix epoch. Release lanes pin build_epoch.source_date_epoch to this value.

tree_hash.description: VCS tree hash of the commit's root tree. Present when tree_state_class is clean_checkout, dirty_local, patched_from_clean, or shallow_clone_accepted. Absent for archive_export_no_git.

commit_digest.description: Commit / tree digest pair. The record carries both the full and short hash so human-readable rendering does not have to re-derive the short form.

host_triple.description: rustc -vV host triple of the machine that ran the build.

llvm_version_ref.description: Opaque ref to the LLVM version string rustc linked against. Null when the LLVM version is implied by rustc_version alone.

toolchain_pin_digest.description: Content digest of rust-toolchain.toml. Used to detect a bumped pin between two otherwise-identical identities.

lockfile_digest.description: Content digest of Cargo.lock. Used to detect a dependency-graph drift between two otherwise-identical identities.

toolchain_pin.description: Toolchain-pin record. Pairs the four reproducible-build-baseline pinned files (rust-toolchain.toml, .cargo/config.toml, Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock) with the cargo / rustc runtime versions the build actually resolved.

target_triple.description: Target triple the artifact was built for (e.g. aarch64-apple-darwin). Defaults to host_triple when no cross-target was specified.

libc_or_runtime_ref.description: Opaque ref to the libc / runtime floor (e.g. glibc 2.31, musl 1.2, Windows CRT pin). Null for cpu_agnostic / OS-agnostic artifact families.

target_descriptor.description: Target descriptor. Carries both the triple string and the closed-vocabulary axes so a parity audit can compare two builds mechanically without parsing the triple.

opt_level_ref.description: Opaque ref to the opt-level token the profile resolved to (e.g. 'opt_level:0', 'opt_level:3', 'opt_level:z'). Opaque so that future opt-level strings do not bump the schema.

profile_descriptor.description: Profile descriptor. Splits the cargo profile into the five independently-addressable axes every downstream lane diffs on (opt level, debug info, panic, strip, profile name).

source_date_epoch.description: Deterministic build epoch. Seconds since the Unix epoch.

build_timestamp_utc.description: ISO-8601 UTC rendering of source_date_epoch. The record carries both the integer and the rendered string so human-readable surfaces do not have to re-format.

build_epoch.description: Build-epoch envelope. Names how the timestamp was derived so a reproducibility verifier can reject a release lane that pinned a manual epoch.

producer_identifier_ref.description: Opaque identifier for the producer. For developer_local this resolves to a workstation pseudonym. For CI lanes this resolves to the CI job identity. For reproduced_clean_room / offline_mirror_rebuild this resolves to the verifier identity.

rebuild_of_identity_ref.description: Opaque pin to the prior exact_build_identity_ref that this lane is reproducing. Null for non-rebuild lanes. MUST be non-null when lane_class is reproduced_clean_room or offline_mirror_rebuild.

producer_lane.description: Producer-lane envelope. Pins which lane minted the identity, who the producer was, and (for verifier lanes) which prior identity this rebuild is of.

attestation_predicate_refs.description: Opaque refs to attestation predicates this identity is covered by (e.g. SLSA provenance, build-provenance, reproducible-build attestation). May be empty only on unsigned_development_only identities.

sbom_document_ref.description: Opaque ref to the SBOM document covering this artifact. Null only on unsigned_development_only identities. Required on every release lane.

provenance_statement_ref.description: Opaque ref to the build-provenance statement. Null only on unsigned_development_only identities.

transparency_log_ref.description: Opaque ref to the transparency-log entry the signature is anchored in. Null only on unsigned_development_only and ephemeral_developer_signature identities.

provenance_linkage.description: Provenance / signing linkage. Final signing and notarization workflows are out of scope at this freeze; the record pins only the typed refs every downstream lane resolves. signing_material_state is the only gate that clears release-evidence publication.

build_log_ref.description: Opaque ref to the build log. Null only on developer_local lanes where no log is retained.

reproducibility_pack_ref.description: Opaque ref to the reproducibility pack (milestones doc 'reproducibility pack'). Required on every release lane.

artifact_graph_node_ref.description: Opaque ref to the artifact-graph node that represents this (identity, artifact family) pair. Required on every non-dev lane; the technical-architecture requirement is that the artifact graph point at a single build identity.

support_bundle_anchor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the support-bundle anchor where this identity is referenced. Present on identities that ship inside a support export.

help_about_payload_ref.description: Opaque ref to the Help / About about_packet_publisher payload that renders this identity. Present on identities that back a running ide_binary / cli_binary.

public_release_note_ref.description: Opaque ref to the public release-note row that announces this identity. Required on every release lane; null on pre-release and dev lanes.

split_symbols_ref.description: Opaque ref to the *_debug_symbols identity that carries the split debug info for this artifact. Required when profile_descriptor.debug_info_class is split_debug_info_external.

source_map_manifest_ref.description: Opaque ref to the source_map_bundle identity that pairs sources to compiled output. Present on identities that ship a renderer bundle.

evidence_linkage.description: Evidence-linkage envelope. Every identity points back to the lanes that consume it: binaries, symbols, source maps, artifact graph node, support bundle, Help / About payload, public release note. Raw bodies never appear in this record; each field is an opaque ref a resolver turns into the underlying artifact under the redaction envelope named on redaction_class.

symbol_tag_refs.description: Opaque refs to build-id / symbol-tag rows that carry the exact_build_identity_ref as a signed label on the emitted binary or symbol archive. Required non-empty when binary_embed_class is embedded_in_binary or sidecar_manifest_beside_binary; may be empty for registry-only families.

propagation_envelope.description: Propagation envelope. Names how the identity is carried physically with the artifact (embedded, sidecar, registry-only, archive-manifest) so crash symbolication and About / Help can resolve a running binary back to one identity.

exact_build_identity_ref.description: Opaque, globally-unique pin for this identity. Downstream records (docs_pack_manifest.running_build_identity_ref, help_status_badge_record.running_build_identity_ref, search_result_truth_record.running_build_identity_ref, evidence_packet fields, support-bundle anchors) MUST resolve to this value field-for-field. No parallel 'build version' string is admissible anywhere else.

workspace_version.description: [workspace.package].version in the root Cargo.toml. The rendered display version. MUST match semver_core.major.minor.patch plus the semver_prerelease_ids (when non-empty) joined by '.' with a leading '-' and the semver_build_metadata joined by '.' with a leading '+'.

semver_core.description: Structured split of the semver core. Required so parity audits can diff major / minor / patch axes mechanically rather than string-parsing workspace_version.

semver_prerelease_ids.description: Ordered list of semver pre-release identifiers (the dotted tokens after the '-'). Empty on stable / lts / hotfix release channels when the release is GA.

semver_build_metadata.description: Ordered list of semver build-metadata tokens (the dotted tokens after the '+'). MUST NOT include wall-clock timestamps; the committer timestamp lives in build_epoch.source_date_epoch. Used for producer-lane tagging (e.g. 'ci.release', 'repro.clean-room').

tree_state_rejected_reasons.description: Ordered list of rejected-tree-state reasons. MUST be empty when tree_state_class is clean_checkout and when the producer lane tolerates the state. MUST be non-empty when a release lane (ci_release, ci_hotfix, reproduced_clean_room, offline_mirror_rebuild) observes a tree_state_class other than clean_checkout or patched_from_clean.

exact_build_identity_record.description: Exact-build identity record. One record per (exact_build_identity_ref, artifact_family_class) pair. A single release cuts N records (one per artifact family) that share commit, tree state, toolchain, target axis, build epoch, producer lane, and provenance, and differ only on artifact_family_class, profile / strip / debug-info axis, propagation envelope, and evidence linkage. This is the super-set every downstream running_build_identity_ref resolves to.
